[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [94mdef[39;49;00m[90m [39;49;00m[92mhistogramdd[39;49;00m(sample, bins=[94m10[39;49;00m, [96mrange[39;49;00m=[94mNone[39;49;00m, density=[94mNone[39;49;00m, weights=[94mNone[39;49;00m):[90m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [90m    [39;49;00m[33m"""[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Compute the multidimensional histogram of some data.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Parameters[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    ----------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    sample : (N, D) array, or (N, D) array_like[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        The data to be histogrammed.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        Note the unusual interpretation of sample when an array_like:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * When an array, each row is a coordinate in a D-dimensional space -[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          such as ``histogramdd(np.array([p1, p2, p3]))``.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * When an array_like, each element is the list of values for single[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          coordinate - such as ``histogramdd((X, Y, Z))``.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        The first form should be preferred.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    bins : sequence or int, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        The bin specification:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * A sequence of arrays describing the monotonically increasing bin[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          edges along each dimension.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * The number of bins for each dimension (nx, ny, ... =bins)[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * The number of bins for all dimensions (nx=ny=...=bins).[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    range : sequence, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        A sequence of length D, each an optional (lower, upper) tuple giving[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        the outer bin edges to be used if the edges are not given explicitly in[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        `bins`.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        An entry of None in the sequence results in the minimum and maximum[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        values being used for the corresponding dimension.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        The default, None, is equivalent to passing a tuple of D None values.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    density : bool, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        If False, the default, returns the number of samples in each bin.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        If True, returns the probability *density* function at the bin,[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``bin_count / sample_count / bin_volume``.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    weights : (N,) array_like, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        An array of values `w_i` weighing each sample `(x_i, y_i, z_i, ...)`.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        Weights are normalized to 1 if density is True. If density is False,[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        the values of the returned histogram are equal to the sum of the[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        weights belonging to the samples falling into each bin.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Returns[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    -------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    H : ndarray[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        The multidimensional histogram of sample x. See density and weights[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        for the different possible semantics.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    edges : tuple of ndarrays[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        A tuple of D arrays describing the bin edges for each dimension.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    See Also[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    --------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    histogram: 1-D histogram[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    histogram2d: 2-D histogram[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Examples[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    --------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> import numpy as np[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> rng = np.random.default_rng()[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> r = rng.normal(size=(100,3))[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> H, edges = np.histogramdd(r, bins = (5, 8, 4))[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> H.shape, edges[0].size, edges[1].size, edges[2].size[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [90m    [39;49;00m[33m"""[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Save an array to a text file.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Parameters[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    ----------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    fname : filename, file handle or pathlib.Path[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        If the filename ends in ``.gz``, the file is automatically saved in[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        compressed gzip format.  `loadtxt` understands gzipped files[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        transparently.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    X : 1D or 2D array_like[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        Data to be saved to a text file.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    fmt : str or sequence of strs, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        A single format (%10.5f), a sequence of formats, or a[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        multi-format string, e.g. 'Iteration %d -- %10.5f', in which[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        case `delimiter` is ignored. For complex `X`, the legal options[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        for `fmt` are:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * a single specifier, ``fmt='%.4e'``, resulting in numbers formatted[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          like ``' (%s+%sj)' % (fmt, fmt)``[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * a full string specifying every real and imaginary part, e.g.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          ``' %.4e %+.4ej %.4e %+.4ej %.4e %+.4ej'`` for 3 columns[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * a list of specifiers, one per column - in this case, the real[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          and imaginary part must have separate specifiers,[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          e.g. ``['%.3e + %.3ej', '(%.15e%+.15ej)']`` for 2 columns[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    delimiter : str, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        String or character separating columns.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    newline : str, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        String or character separating lines.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    header : str, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        String that will be written at the beginning of the file.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    footer : str, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        String that will be written at the end of the file.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    comments : str, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        String that will be prepended to the ``header`` and ``footer`` strings,[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        to mark them as comments. Default: '# ',  as expected by e.g.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``numpy.loadtxt``.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    encoding : {None, str}, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        Encoding used to encode the outputfile. Does not apply to output[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        streams. If the encoding is something other than 'bytes' or 'latin1'[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        you will not be able to load the file in NumPy versions < 1.14. Default[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        is 'latin1'.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    --------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    save : Save an array to a binary file in NumPy ``.npy`` format[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Notes[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    -----[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Further explanation of the `fmt` parameter[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    (``%[flag]width[.precision]specifier``):[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    flags:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``-`` : left justify[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``+`` : Forces to precede result with + or -.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``0`` : Left pad the number with zeros instead of space (see width).[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    width:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        Minimum number of characters to be printed. The value is not truncated[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        if it has more characters.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    precision:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For integer specifiers (eg. ``d,i,o,x``), the minimum number of[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          digits.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``e, E`` and ``f`` specifiers, the number of digits to print[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          after the decimal point.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``g`` and ``G``, the maximum number of significant digits.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``s``, the maximum number of characters.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    specifiers:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``c`` : character[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``d`` or ``i`` : signed decimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``e`` or ``E`` : scientific notation with ``e`` or ``E``.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``f`` : decimal floating point[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``g,G`` : use the shorter of ``e,E`` or ``f``[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``o`` : signed octal[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``s`` : string of characters[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``u`` : unsigned decimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``x,X`` : unsigned hexadecimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    This explanation of ``fmt`` is not complete, for an exhaustive[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    specification see [1]_.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    References[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    ----------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    .. [1] `Format Specification Mini-Language[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m           <https://docs.python.org/library/string.html#format-specification-mini-language>`_,[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m           Python Documentation.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Examples[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    --------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> import numpy as np[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> x = y = z = np.arange(0.0,5.0,1.0)[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> np.savetxt('test.out', x, fmt='%1.4e')   # use exponential notation[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        transparently.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    X : 1D or 2D array_like[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        Data to be saved to a text file.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    fmt : str or sequence of strs, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        A single format (%10.5f), a sequence of formats, or a[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        multi-format string, e.g. 'Iteration %d -- %10.5f', in which[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        case `delimiter` is ignored. For complex `X`, the legal options[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        for `fmt` are:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * a single specifier, ``fmt='%.4e'``, resulting in numbers formatted[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          like ``' (%s+%sj)' % (fmt, fmt)``[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * a full string specifying every real and imaginary part, e.g.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          ``' %.4e %+.4ej %.4e %+.4ej %.4e %+.4ej'`` for 3 columns[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * a list of specifiers, one per column - in this case, the real[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          and imaginary part must have separate specifiers,[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          e.g. ``['%.3e + %.3ej', '(%.15e%+.15ej)']`` for 2 columns[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Notes[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    -----[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Further explanation of the `fmt` parameter[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    (``%[flag]width[.precision]specifier``):[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    flags:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``-`` : left justify[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``+`` : Forces to precede result with + or -.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``0`` : Left pad the number with zeros instead of space (see width).[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    width:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        Minimum number of characters to be printed. The value is not truncated[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        if it has more characters.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    precision:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For integer specifiers (eg. ``d,i,o,x``), the minimum number of[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          digits.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``e, E`` and ``f`` specifiers, the number of digits to print[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          after the decimal point.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``g`` and ``G``, the maximum number of significant digits.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``s``, the maximum number of characters.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    specifiers:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``c`` : character[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``d`` or ``i`` : signed decimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``e`` or ``E`` : scientific notation with ``e`` or ``E``.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``f`` : decimal floating point[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``g,G`` : use the shorter of ``e,E`` or ``f``[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``o`` : signed octal[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``s`` : string of characters[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``u`` : unsigned decimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``x,X`` : unsigned hexadecimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    This explanation of ``fmt`` is not complete, for an exhaustive[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    specification see [1]_.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    References[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    ----------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    .. [1] `Format Specification Mini-Language[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m           <https://docs.python.org/library/string.html#format-specification-mini-language>`_,[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m           Python Documentation.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Examples[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    --------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> import numpy as np[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        case `delimiter` is ignored. For complex `X`, the legal options[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        for `fmt` are:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * a full string specifying every real and imaginary part, e.g.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          ``' %.4e %+.4ej %.4e %+.4ej %.4e %+.4ej'`` for 3 columns[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          and imaginary part must have separate specifiers,[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    (``%[flag]width[.precision]specifier``):[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    flags:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``-`` : left justify[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``+`` : Forces to precede result with + or -.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``0`` : Left pad the number with zeros instead of space (see width).[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    width:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        Minimum number of characters to be printed. The value is not truncated[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        if it has more characters.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    precision:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For integer specifiers (eg. ``d,i,o,x``), the minimum number of[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          digits.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``e, E`` and ``f`` specifiers, the number of digits to print[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          after the decimal point.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``g`` and ``G``, the maximum number of significant digits.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``s``, the maximum number of characters.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    specifiers:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``c`` : character[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``d`` or ``i`` : signed decimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``e`` or ``E`` : scientific notation with ``e`` or ``E``.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``f`` : decimal floating point[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``g,G`` : use the shorter of ``e,E`` or ``f``[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``o`` : signed octal[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``s`` : string of characters[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``u`` : unsigned decimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``x,X`` : unsigned hexadecimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    This explanation of ``fmt`` is not complete, for an exhaustive[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    specification see [1]_.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [90m    [39;49;00m[33m"""[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Save an array to a text file.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Parameters[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    ----------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    fname : filename, file handle or pathlib.Path[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        If the filename ends in ``.gz``, the file is automatically saved in[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        compressed gzip format.  `loadtxt` understands gzipped files[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        transparently.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    X : 1D or 2D array_like[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        Data to be saved to a text file.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    fmt : str or sequence of strs, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        A single format (%10.5f), a sequence of formats, or a[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        multi-format string, e.g. 'Iteration %d -- %10.5f', in which[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        case `delimiter` is ignored. For complex `X`, the legal options[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        for `fmt` are:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * a single specifier, ``fmt='%.4e'``, resulting in numbers formatted[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          like ``' (%s+%sj)' % (fmt, fmt)``[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * a full string specifying every real and imaginary part, e.g.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          ``' %.4e %+.4ej %.4e %+.4ej %.4e %+.4ej'`` for 3 columns[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * a list of specifiers, one per column - in this case, the real[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          and imaginary part must have separate specifiers,[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          e.g. ``['%.3e + %.3ej', '(%.15e%+.15ej)']`` for 2 columns[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    delimiter : str, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        String or character separating columns.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    newline : str, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        String or character separating lines.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    header : str, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        String that will be written at the beginning of the file.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    footer : str, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        String that will be written at the end of the file.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    comments : str, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        String that will be prepended to the ``header`` and ``footer`` strings,[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        to mark them as comments. Default: '# ',  as expected by e.g.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``numpy.loadtxt``.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    encoding : {None, str}, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        Encoding used to encode the outputfile. Does not apply to output[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        streams. If the encoding is something other than 'bytes' or 'latin1'[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        you will not be able to load the file in NumPy versions < 1.14. Default[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        is 'latin1'.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    See Also[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    --------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    save : Save an array to a binary file in NumPy ``.npy`` format[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    savez : Save several arrays into an uncompressed ``.npz`` archive[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    savez_compressed : Save several arrays into a compressed ``.npz`` archive[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Notes[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    -----[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Further explanation of the `fmt` parameter[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    (``%[flag]width[.precision]specifier``):[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    flags:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``-`` : left justify[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``+`` : Forces to precede result with + or -.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``0`` : Left pad the number with zeros instead of space (see width).[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    width:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        Minimum number of characters to be printed. The value is not truncated[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        if it has more characters.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    precision:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For integer specifiers (eg. ``d,i,o,x``), the minimum number of[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          digits.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``e, E`` and ``f`` specifiers, the number of digits to print[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          after the decimal point.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``g`` and ``G``, the maximum number of significant digits.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``s``, the maximum number of characters.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    specifiers:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``c`` : character[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``d`` or ``i`` : signed decimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``e`` or ``E`` : scientific notation with ``e`` or ``E``.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``f`` : decimal floating point[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``g,G`` : use the shorter of ``e,E`` or ``f``[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``o`` : signed octal[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``s`` : string of characters[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``u`` : unsigned decimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``x,X`` : unsigned hexadecimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    This explanation of ``fmt`` is not complete, for an exhaustive[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    specification see [1]_.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    References[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    ----------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    .. [1] `Format Specification Mini-Language[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m           <https://docs.python.org/library/string.html#format-specification-mini-language>`_,[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m           Python Documentation.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Examples[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    --------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> import numpy as np[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> x = y = z = np.arange(0.0,5.0,1.0)[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> np.savetxt('test.out', x, delimiter=',')   # X is an array[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> np.savetxt('test.out', (x,y,z))   # x,y,z equal sized 1D arrays[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> np.savetxt('test.out', x, fmt='%1.4e')   # use exponential notation[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Parameters[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    ----------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    fname : filename, file handle or pathlib.Path[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        If the filename ends in ``.gz``, the file is automatically saved in[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        compressed gzip format.  `loadtxt` understands gzipped files[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        transparently.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    X : 1D or 2D array_like[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        Data to be saved to a text file.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    fmt : str or sequence of strs, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        A single format (%10.5f), a sequence of formats, or a[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        multi-format string, e.g. 'Iteration %d -- %10.5f', in which[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        case `delimiter` is ignored. For complex `X`, the legal options[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        for `fmt` are:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * a single specifier, ``fmt='%.4e'``, resulting in numbers formatted[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          like ``' (%s+%sj)' % (fmt, fmt)``[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * a full string specifying every real and imaginary part, e.g.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          ``' %.4e %+.4ej %.4e %+.4ej %.4e %+.4ej'`` for 3 columns[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        * a list of specifiers, one per column - in this case, the real[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          and imaginary part must have separate specifiers,[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          e.g. ``['%.3e + %.3ej', '(%.15e%+.15ej)']`` for 2 columns[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    delimiter : str, optional[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        String or character separating columns.[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        streams. If the encoding is something other than 'bytes' or 'latin1'[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Notes[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    -----[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Further explanation of the `fmt` parameter[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    (``%[flag]width[.precision]specifier``):[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    flags:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``-`` : left justify[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``+`` : Forces to precede result with + or -.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``0`` : Left pad the number with zeros instead of space (see width).[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    width:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        Minimum number of characters to be printed. The value is not truncated[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        if it has more characters.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    precision:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For integer specifiers (eg. ``d,i,o,x``), the minimum number of[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          digits.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``e, E`` and ``f`` specifiers, the number of digits to print[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          after the decimal point.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``g`` and ``G``, the maximum number of significant digits.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``s``, the maximum number of characters.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    specifiers:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``c`` : character[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``d`` or ``i`` : signed decimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``e`` or ``E`` : scientific notation with ``e`` or ``E``.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``f`` : decimal floating point[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``g,G`` : use the shorter of ``e,E`` or ``f``[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``o`` : signed octal[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``s`` : string of characters[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``u`` : unsigned decimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``x,X`` : unsigned hexadecimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    This explanation of ``fmt`` is not complete, for an exhaustive[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    specification see [1]_.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    References[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    ----------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    .. [1] `Format Specification Mini-Language[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m           <https://docs.python.org/library/string.html#format-specification-mini-language>`_,[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m           Python Documentation.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Examples[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    --------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> import numpy as np[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> x = y = z = np.arange(0.0,5.0,1.0)[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> np.savetxt('test.out', x, delimiter=',')   # X is an array[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> np.savetxt('test.out', (x,y,z))   # x,y,z equal sized 1D arrays[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> np.savetxt('test.out', x, fmt='%1.4e')   # use exponential notation[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m

[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    --------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    save : Save an array to a binary file in NumPy ``.npy`` format[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    savez : Save several arrays into an uncompressed ``.npz`` archive[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    savez_compressed : Save several arrays into a compressed ``.npz`` archive[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Notes[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    -----[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Further explanation of the `fmt` parameter[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    (``%[flag]width[.precision]specifier``):[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    flags:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``-`` : left justify[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``+`` : Forces to precede result with + or -.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``0`` : Left pad the number with zeros instead of space (see width).[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    width:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        Minimum number of characters to be printed. The value is not truncated[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        if it has more characters.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    precision:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For integer specifiers (eg. ``d,i,o,x``), the minimum number of[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          digits.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``e, E`` and ``f`` specifiers, the number of digits to print[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m          after the decimal point.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``g`` and ``G``, the maximum number of significant digits.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        - For ``s``, the maximum number of characters.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    specifiers:[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``c`` : character[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``d`` or ``i`` : signed decimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``e`` or ``E`` : scientific notation with ``e`` or ``E``.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``f`` : decimal floating point[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``g,G`` : use the shorter of ``e,E`` or ``f``[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``o`` : signed octal[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``s`` : string of characters[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``u`` : unsigned decimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m        ``x,X`` : unsigned hexadecimal integer[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    This explanation of ``fmt`` is not complete, for an exhaustive[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    specification see [1]_.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    References[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    ----------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    .. [1] `Format Specification Mini-Language[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m           <https://docs.python.org/library/string.html#format-specification-mini-language>`_,[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m           Python Documentation.[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    Examples[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    --------[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> import numpy as np[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> x = y = z = np.arange(0.0,5.0,1.0)[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> np.savetxt('test.out', x, delimiter=',')   # X is an array[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> np.savetxt('test.out', (x,y,z))   # x,y,z equal sized 1D arrays[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m    >>> np.savetxt('test.out', x, fmt='%1.4e')   # use exponential notation[39;49;00m
[Linux tests/smoke_test]   |     [33m[39;49;00m
